name: William Wight's Chipotle Chiles
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the smoker by setting it up with 5 lbs of self-lighting briquettes in the bottom and a water pan filled with cold water above the charcoal.
2. Soak mesquite wood chips for an hour or until they are saturated with water.
3. Prepare fresh whole pork loin, cutting it into two halves. Rub each half with your preferred smoker spice blend.
4. Gather additional smoked ingredients: 3 lbs of mesquite wood chips (soaked), and equal amounts of Jalapenos and TAM Jalapeno peppers (about 2 lbs total).
5. Place the prepared meat on the lower shelf of the smoker, and arrange about two layers of jalapeno peppers on the upper shelf.
6. Begin smoking by adding a few mesquite chips to the charcoal bed every 15 minutes as needed to maintain smoke production.
7. Smoke the pork loin for approximately 3 hours (or until fully cooked), then remove it from the smoker.
8. After smoking, place the smoked jalapeno peppers on foil-lined cookie trays in an oven preheated to its lowest setting. Allow them to dry completely for about 24 hours.
9. Once dried and cooled, blend the peppers into fine flakes or powder using a blender.
10. Combine all the powdered chipotle peppers together, which should yield approximately 3/4 quart.
My powdered chipotles have a rich smoky flavor with a mild heat level. They can be used on tacos or sprinkled over cream cheese spread on crackers for an enhanced taste experience. Enjoy these flavorful chiles until next season!
